title: allow Write(*) + deny Write(*) -> deny wins wherever it matches; Edit is covered by neither (stays ask)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p2b-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

First use the Read tool to read $CASE_DIR/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $CASE_DIR/note.txt. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool calls.

hypothesis: deny is evaluated before allow; deny wins wherever it matches, and the match scope of Write(*) is measured across cwd / $HOME / subdir. Rules are tool-scoped: Edit is covered by neither allow nor deny, so it falls back to default ask. Uses the WORKING Write(*) form (Write(**) and path-specific forms silently fail to match -- see P3).
